sentences.text: Good afternoon, everybody.

Thanks.
Sure thing.
Hope everyone's having a great day.
All right, looks like we're actually, we've got a bunch of folks in here already.
So I think we'll wait another maybe 30 seconds, but just some introductions here.
Uh My name is Phil.
I'm the customer success manager who's been working with the leadership team at Willow Bridge to help get Apartment IQ set up across the portfolio.
Really excited to walk you folks through Apartment IQ today.
I'd love for this to be as participatory as possible.
So as I'm going through any of the screens, feel free to come on mute and ask me any questions that you have as we're working through some of these areas in apartment IQ.
Um
And if anybody has a specific market survey or a specific zip code that they work with, feel free to shout it out and we can use that as our example as you walk through
should we use?

Thank you, Brianna.
All right.
So for those of us who don't know 100% what apartment IQ all is all about, I'll just take a quick step back.
Apartment IQ is a software that presently relies on 100% publicly available information.
No private information, no integrations with P MS of any kind.
Uh We use the landlord's public facing website as the source of truth.
That's how we pull all our data and information that gives rise to the market surveys and data that you're seeing here.
We've been in business in this iteration for a little over four or five years now.
So we've got a lot of history.
We've looked up a lot of things.
Our database is pretty deep, which has been able to give us rise to a lot of historical information, uh and give us a really comprehensive view of the market.
We're currently tracking over 32 million units in the United States.
When you log into apartment IQ, this will be your landing page.

We'll start learning about what a market survey is and then we'll talk about some other tools like our on demand research reports which allows you to create a market survey report for any property, whether you own it or not really helpful for folks who are pitching your business, really helpful for folks who are learning about a potential new building that doesn't even exist yet and want to develop kind of comps for that.
And our explore page which will further allow folks to learn about different zip codes submarkets and understand what the trending uh information is there
popping into this whitlow market.
The default page opens up to our digitized market survey.
You'll notice tabs across the top which are our main navigation tabs for the other key reports.

a variety of filters for different attributes.
If we're looking to create a specific kind of view in our market, we want to sort out certain sizes perhaps or specific rental values, even unit style.

How do you determine that similarity score?
What is that?
What makes that similarity score?
Yeah, great question Brianna.
That is based on the subject properties, bed type pricing and sizing the year built in any renovations that have occurred, uh total unit accounts and then a distance radius to other properties.
So by default, we have some presets for that.
